ideation: This animation sequence simulates the action of pressing a play/pause button.  It will use a combination of both arms to represent the pushing and releasing of a hypothetical button. The sequence starts with both arms raised to shoulder height in a neutral position. Then, the right arm moves forward to simulate pressing a button. After a slight pause, the right arm returns to the neutral position, completing the animation. The left arm provides contrast and balance. Frame 1 shows both arms in a neutral position. Frame 2 shows the right arm moving forward and the left arm slightly shifting. Frame 3 depicts the right arm pressing the button, while frame 4 shows the right arm returning to neutral and frame 5 shows both arms resuming their neutral positions.

frames.beat: both arms rest, ready for action
right arm prepares to press, left arm balances
the right arm pushes forward, pressing the button
right arm retracts slightly, releasing pressure
both arms return to a neutral, resting state
